description_long: This object is a preserved crustacean, likely a crayfish or small lobster, displayed on a flat white surface. Its body is segmented into a robust cephalothorax and a tapered abdomen, with a hard exoskeleton that is mostly brownish-orange in color. The most prominent features are its two large, asymmetrical claws (chelae), which are dark greenish-brown and extend far in front of the body. The crustacean has multiple slender walking legs and two long, thin antennae that extend outward from the head. The eyes are small and positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax. The overall appearance suggests a natural specimen, possibly used for educational or display purposes in an urban context.
description: A brown-orange crustacean with large, dark claws, segmented body, long antennae, and multiple legs, displayed on a white surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws extended, and the segmentation between cephalothorax and abdomen.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the claws, the segmentation of the body, and the spread of the antennae.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the claws and body profile visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, the head, and the forward-facing antennae.
